description: Configure Stellarium to simulate the Hawaiian night sky for a deep-sea departure from Hilo, Hawaii (19.72° N, 155.09° W, Altitude 0m) on May 16, 2024, at 08:00 UTC. Apply the 'Hawaiian Starlines' sky culture and configure the display to simulate a traditional navigator's view at sea by enabling the atmosphere, azimuthal grid, and constellation lines and labels, while disabling the ground/landscape. Document the positions of two critical guiding stars, Arcturus and Acrux, by centering the view on each and capturing screenshots to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Create a training document at ~/Desktop/wayfinding_guide.txt containing the departure location, the date and UTC time used, the sky culture applied, and a brief note identifying Arcturus and Acrux as the targets documented in the screenshots.
